A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

ROBERT C. PUNSTEN

STEPHEN M. MCNAMARA
GARY A. PEMBERTON
WAYNE C. BROUGH
CINDY R. HUGHES
DENISE E. HARBAUGH
MONICA E. GOTT
ERIC T. SALTZMAN
MICHAEL J. RUBINO
STEVEN H. ROTH
GARY R. DOWNS
JOHN O. IRELAND
NICHOLAS J. COLBOURN
DAVID H. MANN
GALE L. SCHLESINGER
JOHN H. ZERMON
DARRYL S. GIBSON

ATTORNEYS AT LAW
660 NEWPORT CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 1600

POST OFFICE BOX 7680

NEWPORT BEACH, CALIFORNIA 92660-8441

TELEPHONE (714) 725-4000

TELECOPIER (714) 640-7035

FAX NUMBER
(714) 725-4100

JOHN E. BRECKEMRIDGE

RENA G. STONE

OF COUNSEL

WRITER'S DIRECT DIAL

March 27, 1990

Kathryne Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814


Dear Ms. Donovan:

On behalf of William Pursley, City Councilmember of the
City of Lancaster, the undersigned requests the FPPC to provide
formal written advice pursuant to Government Code Section
83114(b) regarding Mr. Pursley, who is also a member of the
governing board of the Lancaster Redevelopment Agency (the
"Agency"). The City Council of the City of Lancaster and the
Agency have recently approved a Disposition and Development
Agreement (DDA) between the Agency and the Lancaster Business
Park Limited, providing for redevelopment of a site (the
"Site") located within two thousand (2,000) feet of real
property owned by Councilmember Pursley. The Agency and the
City Council approved the DDA on February 20, 1990 by
Resolution No. 1-90 and Resolution 90-55, respectively.
Councilmember Pursley abstained from participating in any
discussions and decisions in connection with approval of the
DDA. These resolutions are enclosed for your review.

The purpose of the DDA is to implement the Redevelopment
Plan for the Amargosa Redevelopment Project Area which includes
the Site. The Site consists of 160 acres of undeveloped land
belonging to approximately sixty-two (62) owners. The land use
designation in the General Plan for the Site is industrial and
the property is currently zoned MPD (manufacturing planned
development).

Kathryne Donovan, General Counsel
March 27, 1990
Page 2


Under the terms of the DDA, the Agency would be responsible for
acquiring the individual parcels comprising the Site which are
not currently owned by the limited partnership. Upon
acquisition, the Site would be transferred to the partnership
as a single unit for development as improved lots for a
Business/Industrial Park.

According to the Statement of Economic Interest filed by
Councilmember Pursley on April 6, 1989, Councilmember Pursley
holds an interest in real property located on Sahuayo Street
(the "Pursley Property"), which is within two thousand (2,000)
feet of the Site. The Pursley Property is worth more than
$10,000 and less than $100,000 and is currently unimproved.
The land use designation of the Pursley Property in the General
Plan for the City of Lancaster is industrial and the property
is currently zoned "Specific Plan 80-02" (industrial uses).
For your reference the enclosed map illustrates the location of
the Pursley Property in relationship to the boundaries of the
Site. Also enclosed is an aerial photograph taken in December
1988 which shows the development of surrounding property. As
indicated on the map and the photograph, the Pursley Property
is geographically separated from the Site by industrial
properties.

Based upon the distance between the Pursley Property and
the Site and the fact that these properties are geographically
separated by industrial properties, an independent appraiser
has determined that redevelopment of the Site pursuant to the
DDA would not have a financial effect on the Pursley Property
equal to or greater than the threshold ($10,000) established by
title 2, Cal. Code of Regulations, Section 18702.3(a)(3) for a
disqualifying conflict of interest. The City of Lancaster
contacted as appraiser, Mr. Daniel Johnson, to obtain an
evaluation concerning the change in value, if any, that
Councilmember Pursley might expect in the Pursley Property due
to redevelopment of the Site pursuant to the DDA. Enclosed
please find a copy of a letter dated March 26, 1990 in which
Mr. Johnson expresses his opinion with respect to the financial
effects of redevelopment of the Site pursuant to the DDA upon
the Pursley Property.

Based upon the foregoing material facts, I request a
response from the FPPC to the following question:

May Councilmember/Agency member Pursley participate in any
decisions before the City Council or Agency in connection with
implementation of the DDA including:

Kathyrne Donovan, General Counsel
March 27, 1990
Page 3


     (a) Decisions involving the acquisition of the site
including the use of the Agency's power of eminent domain to
acquire the Site;

     (b) Decisions involving the approval of drawings and
plans for development of the Site; and

     (c) Decisions involving land use matters in which the
developer is an applicant.

If you desire any further information, please contact me at
(714) 725-4162.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        STRADING/MOCCA, CARLSON & RAUTH


                                        [signature]
                                        Dave R. McEwen
                                        City Attorney


GIS/ts
0212m

Enclosures:

1.  Map
2.  Aerial Photograph
3.  Resolution No. 1-90
4.  Resolution No. 90-55
5.  Statement of Economic Interest
6.  Appraisal Letter


cc: William Pursley (w/o encl.)
    Steve West (w/o encl.)

sections.question: May Councilmember/Agency member Pursley participate in any decisions before the City Council or Agency in connection with implementation of the DDA including: (a) Decisions involving the acquisition of the site including the use of the Agency's power of eminent domain to acquire the Site; (b) Decisions involving the approval of drawings and plans for development of the Site; and (c) Decisions involving land use matters in which the developer is an applicant.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether City Councilmember and redevelopment agency board member William Pursley can participate in decisions regarding a Disposition and Development Agreement for a site located within 2,000 feet of property he owns, given that an independent appraisal concludes the redevelopment would not cause a financial effect exceeding the $10,000 disqualifying conflict threshold.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from counsel seeking formal written advice under Gov. Code § 83114(b), not an FPPC response. No conclusion/advice section present. Document contains detailed factual background regarding property values, zoning, and independent appraisal to support the request.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: May Councilmember/Agency member Pursley participate in any decisions before the City Council or Agency in connection with implementation of the DDA including: (a) Decisions involving the acquisition of the site including the use of the Agency's power of eminent domain to acquire the Site; (b) Decisions involving the approval of drawings and plans for development of the Site; and (c) Decisions involving land use matters in which the developer is an applicant.

embedding.summary: Attorney for William Pursley, a Lancaster City Councilmember and board member of the Lancaster Redevelopment Agency, requests FPPC advice on whether Pursley can participate in decisions regarding a Disposition and Development Agreement for a redevelopment project site located within 2,000 feet of property Pursley owns, with supporting appraisal indicating no disqualifying financial impact.
